title: 1.1: Reports how shared memory segments are created (e.g., shm.CreateTemp with pattern kssh-PID-, WriteWithSize with 4-byte size header)
1.2: Identifies the central data structure coordinating SSH connection setup (e.g., connection_data struct containing remote_args, host_opts, shm_name fields)
1.3: Explains how the shell integration archive is created (e.g., gzip-compressed tar with data.sh, bash/fish/zsh scripts, terminfo entries)
1.4: Explains how environment variables are serialized (e.g., export statements like export 'TERM'="xterm-kitty", unset commands)
1.5: Explains how connection reuse is determined (e.g., -O check flag to test existing master, ControlMaster=auto option)
1.6: Explains how the bootstrap script is encoded (e.g., single quote becomes vertical tab \v, backslash becomes form feed \f)
1.7: Explains how the terminal communicates with the remote shell (e.g., DCS format ESC P @ kitty-TYPE | BASE64_DATA ESC \, dcs_to_kitty function)
1.8: Identifies the data transfer protocol between kitty and remote bootstrap (e.g., KITTY_DATA_START and KITTY_DATA_END markers, base64 encoded tarfile)
1.9: Reports how shared memory segments are secured (e.g., 0600 permissions, UID/GID verification, one-time access via unlink after read)
